name: Harlows School Bus
owner_or_parent: Hageness family (Harlow H. Hageness, founder; sons Jason, Jeremy, Justin Hageness)
employees_est: 32 (Spokane Valley site only; total enterprise materially larger across 6 states)
districts_or_customers: School transportation contracts ranging from 6 to 100+ routes per location; operates in ND, SD, MT, ID, WA, NE
recent_news: Continues to actively hire drivers and expand routes as of 2025-26; long-standing family-owned regional contractor with ~28 locations across 6 states
fit_notes: HIGH-VALUE TARGET: Spokane Valley address is just one of ~28 Harlow's locations across ND/SD/MT/ID/WA/NE — this is a substantial multi-state family-owned student transportation platform (school bus service + affiliated bus dealership), NOT a single-site sub-$2M operator. Founder Harlow Hageness now ~84; three sons run the business with 25+ years each. Classic second-generation family business with potential succession dynamics; would be a platform-scale acquisition, not a tuck-in. Worth a direct senior conversation.

name: Rides 4 School
fleet_size_est: very small (likely 1-3 vans, not yellow buses)
districts_or_customers: Private pay families in Irving, TX area; not a district contractor — operates as a private parent-pay van shuttle to/from schools paired with a home daycare
recent_news: No M&A or notable news; continues operating as small owner-operated service per 2024-26 web presence (yourchildsride.com, @yourchildsride on X)
fit_notes: LOW FIT. Not a school-district contractor — this is a private-pay van service paired with a home daycare (Tippett's Transportation/Rides4School). Too small and consumer-facing to be a Summit tuck-in; no district contracts, no yellow-bus fleet.

name: Ohio School Bus SVC LLC
owner_or_parent: independent (Jennifer Fisher listed as manager per state filing)
fleet_size_est: small (~10-20 buses implied by 31 employees and ~$1.1M revenue)
districts_or_customers: Likely contracts with one or more Ross County, OH districts (Chillicothe City, Adena Local, Huntington Local, Paint Valley Local, Zane Trace, or Union-Scioto) — not confirmed via public sources
recent_news: No public news found; no website, no press, no social media presence located in search
fit_notes: Possible small local contractor in Ross County, OH but no web footprint located. Generic name and Mt. Tabor Rd rural location suggest a true small family-run operator. Would require phone outreach / district FOIA to confirm operating status, contracts, and ownership before pursuing as tuck-in.

name: VII Equity Bus Resources LLC
owner_or_parent: independent (Valerie S. Lumbus listed as manager; possibly affiliated with her VDL Enterprises, LLC of Ohio)
fleet_size_est: small (~10-15 buses implied by 25 employees and ~$1.1M revenue)
districts_or_customers: Unknown — no public contracts located; greater Cleveland/Solon area suggests possible contracts with Cleveland Metro, Solon, Bedford, Warrensville Heights, or Beachwood districts but not confirmed
recent_news: No M&A or news found. Operator is also linked to VDL Enterprises (Bedford, OH); name 'VII Equity' is unusual and may reflect a holding/financing structure rather than a marketed operating brand
fit_notes: Very low web footprint. Solon address (Aurora Rd) is a light industrial corridor consistent with a small bus yard. Worth a screening call to verify whether they hold any district contracts vs. acting as a leasing/charter shell. Likely too small as a standalone tuck-in unless paired with other NE Ohio operators.

name: Mckenzie School Bus SVC LLC
fleet_size_est: very small (~8-12 buses implied by 14 employees and ~$1.0M revenue)
districts_or_customers: Almost certainly contracts with Allegany County Public Schools (Frostburg area); ACPS uses a network of small independent owner-operator contractors typical of western Maryland
recent_news: Active LLC per Maryland records; related entity 'McKenzie Farm's & Transport LLC' (USDOT 3579094) operates as interstate freight carrier in Frostburg — same family is in trucking as well
fit_notes: Classic small family-owned western Maryland school-bus contractor (Allegany County), with a sibling freight operation. Sub-scale on its own as a tuck-in, but potentially interesting as part of an ACPS-area roll-up of multiple owner-operators. Direct family-owned succession candidate given concentrated ownership under David McKenzie.
